timeline.label: Q2 FY2026 10-Q: six-month revenue $6.78B, net income $2.01B; FCF run-rate ~$8/sh annualized
FY2025 10-K: revenue $11.0B (+17% YoY), OCF $4.81B, FCF $4.28B ($8.61/sh); Industrial 45% of revenue
FY2024 trough year: revenue $9.43B, net income $1.64B as industrial and communications softened
FY2023 peak cycle: revenue $12.31B, net income $3.31B before broad semiconductor correction
Maxim Integrated acquisition closes, doubling analog scale and adding automotive/industrial mix
Shareholders approve Maxim Integrated acquisition; leverage rises to fund analog consolidation
Analog Devices announces agreement to acquire Maxim Integrated Products
Linear Technology acquisition closes, expanding power management and industrial analog portfolio
Company announces agreement to acquire Linear Technology for ~$14.8B
Analog Devices founded in Norwood, Massachusetts; decades-long analog and mixed-signal franchise

industry.structure: Oligopolistic high-performance analog and mixed-signal semiconductors: ADI, Texas Instruments, and NXP compete on design breadth, manufacturing scale, and customer intimacy across industrial, automotive, and communications end markets.
industry.share_shift: ADI gained scale through Linear (2017) and Maxim (2021) integrations; FY2025 revenue recovered to $11.0B with Industrial at 45% and Automotive at 30% of mix.
industry.trend: Electrification, factory automation, and AI data-center power delivery expand analog content per system; cyclical inventory corrections remain the primary near-term swing factor.
